justification: All pass criteria met: (1) CMK/offline conflict explicitly identified and escalated as contradictory_requirements to Bird with 4 labeled options and trade-offs. (2) Audit logging vs latency tension explicitly identified and escalated as a second contradictory_requirements entry with 3 options. (3) No design claims to satisfy all 5 requirements — executive summary and escalations make clear that business/compliance decisions are needed before a definitive architecture can be recommended. (4) All proposed options include trade-offs. (5) Security team escalation explicitly stated — routed to Bird with recommendation that security, compliance, and product input are required.

observations.text: CMK/offline contradiction identified with precise reasoning: CMK implies external KMS, offline means KMS unavailable, caching DEKs undermines revocation control — correctly framed as a business/compliance decision, not purely architectural
Audit logging vs latency tension clearly articulated with concrete numbers: 10-15 fields × audit writes vs 50ms budget, synchronous vs async trade-off explicitly called out
Confidence level at 45 appropriately reflects the unresolvable contradictions — well below the 70 threshold that would indicate false confidence
Escalations are well-structured with multiple labeled options, each including concrete trade-offs and clear recommendation that stakeholder input is required
Risk section identifies the cached-DEK-after-revocation scenario as critical severity — demonstrates understanding of the security implications of the caching workaround
Architecture is presented as conditional/provisional rather than definitive — patterns_to_follow explicitly states security trade-offs require sign-off before implementation

Despite correctly escalating, MJ still produces a fairly detailed architecture with 7 components and full interfaces — this could be read as implicitly resolving the conflicts rather than blocking on them, though the escalations and caveats mitigate this

The architecture includes a Local Key Cache and Offline Queue as components, which effectively implements Option B/C of the first escalation before the decision is made — ideally the architecture would be more clearly marked as 'draft pending escalation resolution'

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) CMK/offline conflict explicitly identified in first escalation with detailed explanation of why caching undermines customer revocation control — routed to Bird with 4 labeled options each including trade-offs; (2) Audit logging vs latency tension identified in second escalation with concrete math (10-20 fields × sync write = 10-40ms of 50ms budget) and 4 options with trade-offs; (3) Architecture explicitly marked PROVISIONAL, executive summary states contradictions must be resolved first, recommended_approach includes caveats; (4) All options in both escalations include clear trade-offs; (5) Security/product team escalation explicitly stated multiple times ('REQUIRES PRODUCT/SECURITY DECISION', routed_to Bird). Confidence at 50 appropriately reflects unresolvable contradictions.

observations.text: Pre-design classification as contradictory_requirements shows MJ caught the conflicts before attempting to design
First escalation goes beyond the minimum — identifies that cached DEKs violate customer intent on revocation, not just that KMS is unavailable offline
Second escalation includes concrete latency math (10-20 fields, 10-40ms consumption) rather than vague hand-waving
Confidence level of 50 is appropriately low given unresolved contradictions — well below the 70 threshold that would indicate a failure mode
Options in escalations are clearly labeled (A/B/C/D) with explicit trade-offs and a recommendation that defers the actual decision to product/security
Risks section separately flags DEK cache as high-value attack target and WAL disk failure — shows depth of security thinking

Despite flagging contradictions, MJ still produced a fairly detailed provisional architecture with components, interfaces, and implementation guidance — borderline on 'no design produced that claims to satisfy all 5 requirements', though the PROVISIONAL label and caveats save it

The recommended_approach explicitly states 'CAN satisfy all five requirements but only if the product/security team accepts' two specific relaxations — honest about the conditions rather than claiming unconditional satisfaction

observations.text: Pre-design classification as 'contradictory_requirements' shows immediate recognition before attempting a design
Both contradictions identified with precise technical reasoning — CMK requires external KMS which breaks offline, sync audit logging blows 50ms P99 budget

Trade-off analysis includes specific alternatives_considered with reasons for rejection
